items.title: The immersive Indigenous language class where English is left at the door

children.content: After learning from Native American Yuchi families, Pertame elders and young mothers in Alice Springs are raising babies in a total immersion Language Nest and hope to extend the model into primary school.

image.altText: A woman plays blocks with a child.

items.title: NT's deputy opposition leader dumped for new recruit

children.content: The Northern Territory Labor party has replaced its deputy opposition leader with a newcomer to parliament, in a move they say will win back the trust and confidence of the people of Darwin.

image.altText: A man wearing a red shirt standing in a shed, looking directly down the camera.

items.title: NT government extends town camp alcohol bans

children.content: The NT government is extending alcohol bans in town camps beyond 2027, in a move welcomed by the health sector as a win for community safety.

gallery.altText: A row of empty liquor bottles lined up on a kitchen bench, the adjoining walls are covered in marks and dents.

thumbnail.altText: A row of empty liquor bottles lined up on a kitchen bench, the adjoining walls are covered in marks and dents.

items.title: In pictures: Alice Springs celebrates 30 years of unique craft festival

children.content: As the Alice Springs Beanie Festival celebrates its 30th and final year, we're exploring the weird and wonderful handmade creations — and the characters working hard behind the scenes — that have made the annual event so special.

image.altText: A man smiling at the camera, on his head is a handmade beanie featuring a knitted monkey holding a banana.

items.title: Call for youth foyer to help tackle homelessness in the NT

children.content: Youth homelessness rates remain stubbornly high in Central Australia and advocates are renewing calls for a transitional housing model for young people to be established to tackle the issue.

image.altText: a pile of pillows on a sandy ground with bushland in the background

items.title: NT government reveals scale of bungled smart meter rollout

children.content: The Northern Territory's main energy retailer, Jacana, reveals about 5,000 customers — thousands more than previously thought — have been caught up in a smart meter bungle leading to long delays in their billing.

image.altText: A smart meter

items.title: Couple forced to live on verandah for three months awaiting flood repairs

children.content: Katherine couple Nicki and Luke Asling have been living on their back verandah for the past three months, after their home was flooded to the ceiling in March. Local renovators say it will take months for all the repairs in town to be completed.

image.altText: A woman sits on a bed outdoors, patting a dog.

items.title: Socceroos quick hits: Selection stunner leaves stars on bench, coach's VAR frustration

children.content: A Cameron Burgess own goal and VAR drama cost Australia against the US, before cramp proved a problem for the match referee. Here are the five quick hits from the Socceroos vs USA.

articles.title: Irankunda bemoans ref 'stinker', Brazil turns on the style against Haiti

children.content: Matheus Cunha scores scores twice for Brazil against Haiti, while Morocco defeats Scotland 1-0 thanks to the fastest goal at this World Cup so far. Follow live.

image.altText: Nestory Irankunda holds his hands on his head in disbelief near the referee

articles.title: 'He got that wrong': Schwarzer says selection mistakes cost Socceroos

children.content: The Socceroos will rue a missed opportunity after a dire first half against the USA, with Tony Popovic's selections at least partly to blame, says Mark Schwarzer.

image.altText: Tony Popovic looks stern

articles.title: How blind faith in the internet helped a man fulfill a World Cup promise

children.content: The general advice is if it seems too good to be true, then it probably is. For Ash Jurberg, a punt on an anonymous message on LinkedIn sent him to the World Cup and fulfilled a 16-year-old promise to his sons.

image.altText: Three men in soccer jersey's pose for a photo

items.title: Socceroos live events marred by flares, alleged sexual assault

children.content: Thousands of people flocked to sites across Australia to show their support as the Socceroos fell short against World Cup host USA.

image.altText: A crowd of people.

items.title: 'Everything's just so wrong': Inside the country town that turned to One Nation

children.content: In just a few short weeks, a small country town became One Nation heartland. It is a blueprint for how the party can turn Australia orange.

image.altText: Older man wearing blue jumper and orange One nation hat stands with arms outstretched in front of service station

items.title: What does it mean for a nation to be a 'monoculture'?

children.content: Pauline Hanson has called for Australia to reject multiculturalism and be "monocultural". So what does it involve and are there any nations overseas that show what it might look like in Australia?

image.altText: People walk across a zebra crossing in a city with billboards in Japanese in the background.

items.title: As Trump praises 'very smart' leaders in Iran deal, Qalibaf is on the rise

children.content: When Iran and the United States inked a deal on the war this week, one unlikely figure on the Iranian side emerged from the shadows of the negotiations and into the open.

image.altText: ranian Supreme leader Ali Khamenei (C) with Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf (L) and Judiciary Chief Mohsen Ejeie (R)

items.title: A Sydney startup built an AI porn empire. There are serious questions about child abuse material

children.content: The ABC traced a Sydney startup to one of the world's biggest AI pornography sites and found what experts say is child abuse material.

image.altText: A montage of profile photos of characters with blurred faces and age estiamtes beside them.

items.title: Mother holds onto hope her son's killer will be found after remote WA murder

children.content: The grieving mother of Josh Warneke, who was found dead on the side of a road in WA's remote Kimberley, has faced a long battle for answers. But how likely is it the 21-year-old's killer will be brought to justice?

image.altText: Image of a photograph of Josh Warneke placed in front of a memorial in Broome, Western Australia.

items.title: Why these retirees won't give up the 3:30am starts and 14-hour shifts

children.content: When the alarm goes off at 3:30am in this freezing outback pit-stop, it's music to the ears of the volunteer bakers who happily spend their winters in a bakery like no other.

gallery.altText: A man in a cap laughing in a kitchen with women laughing behind him.
Two men carry trays of freshly baked bread loaves up steps from an underground baker.

Two men carry trays of freshly baked bread loaves up steps from an underground baker.

items.title: Zombie director Yeon Sang-ho says AI wants to 'fold' us into one

children.content: Train to Busan director Yeon Sang-ho is ringing the warning bell over AI's potential to strip humans of their individuality with his new zombie film Colony.

image.altText: Colony director and writer Yeon Sang-ho.

items.title: If you didn't do it at 40, do it at 60

children.content: For some women, the act of asking, "what might still be possible" — of refusing to accept that the most meaningful chapters are already behind them — might be its own kind of resistance.

image.altText: Tilda Swinton poses on a red carpet wearing a red long sleeve dress and gold choker

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: What Off Campus can teach us about centring women's pleasure

children.content: The college hockey romance has had millions of people swooning over its main characters and the messages around healthy relationships.

image.altText: An image of a hockey player lifting up a woman in an ice rink

items.title: Heart-breaking reason these school kids dress as superheroes

children.content: Nate Lawson's family looked to their son's love of superheroes to raise awareness about the rare disease that took his life.

image.altText: school students sitting on a retaining wall, dressed as superheroes

items.title: How police are unravelling Dezi Freeman's support network

children.content: As police continue the investigation into Dezi Freeman's support network, experts say those who aided him might have shared similar ideology.

gallery.altText: Two police walk holding seized items.

thumbnail.altText: Two police walk holding seized items.

items.title: Not so long ago, politicians cared about the optics of big donations

children.content: One Nation's dramatic return to the national stage seems to have dashed any sense of restraint or shame about ties to wealthy donors.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart and Pauline Hanson 2026-06-18

items.title: So much for the promise that Tasmanians deserve better from politicians

children.content: After last year's snap election, Tasmania's premier declared voters were "sick and tired of the political games". After this week, it is clear that the politicians just cannot help themselves.

image.altText: Jeremy Rockliff leans to his left while seated.

avatar.alt: A woman with glasses stands in a TV studio.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah agree to ceasefire, Trump says everything 'knocked out' in Iran

children.content: Fighting escalated between Israel and Hezbollah in south Lebanon overnight, with reports of 47 people killed in Israeli strikes and four Israeli soldiers killed.

items.title: Why is your phone going off? Don't be alarmed, it's a warning system test

children.content: This week, phones in some regional towns have received a warning text alert and emitted an alarm as part of national warning system testing. Next month, your phone could receive it too.

image.altText: A phone notification with the words 'Priority AusAlert'.

items.title: Family of medical student who survived remote crash advocates for rescue flights

children.content: One minute, Kate Scott was a LifeFlight volunteer. The next, she was a mother dialling Triple Zero. But the service that saved her daughter is facing a shortfall.

image.altText: Lucy Scott wearing a neck brace and unconscious in a hospital bed, her partner lays by her side

items.title: Getting rid of an old fridge? Here's what you need to know

children.content: Chemicals hiding in old fridges and air conditioners can contribute to the greenhouse gas effect if not properly disposed.

items.title: Meloni hits back at Trump's claim she 'begged' him for photo

children.content: The Italian prime minister accused her one-time close ally of fabricating a story about her, after the US President told an Italian TV channel that she had "begged" him to take a photo with her at a G7 summit.

image.altText: Giorgia Meloni and Donald Trump shaking hands.

items.title: UK PM vows to fight for job after rival's stunning by-election win

children.content: Sir Keir Starmer's principal rival, Andy Burnham, has stormed back into parliament after a near-decade absence with a by-election win.

image.altText: A man addresses a crowd.

items.title: 'My jaw kept hitting the floor': Stories emerge from 225yo Sydney home

children.content: When the Tedesco family bought a sprawling property called Chelsea Farm in the Hills Shire district, they knew nothing of its more than 220-year history.

items.title: Qld minister tight-lipped about 'Project Invisibility' Indigenous sackings

children.content: Under the direction of Arts Minister John-Paul Langbroek, eight Indigenous directors have disappeared from the boards of south-east Queensland's cultural institutions.

image.altText: John-Paul Langbroek speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Sisters separated in Afghanistan are becoming Australian citizens

children.content: Five years ago, Adiba was separated from her sister Arya as she fled Afghanistan. Now the Ganji siblings have reunited in Melbourne and are on their way to obtaining Australian citizenship.

image.altText: Adiba and Arya, two sisters, holding hands, smiling

items.title: Peanut industry confidence returns after Crumptons takes over Bega sites

children.content: After being tossed a lifeline last year, Queensland's peanut harvest is in full swing with plans in place to help the industry thrive once again.

image.altText: Peanut farmer crouching down next to his crop.

items.title: Women remaining in abusive relationships over concerns for pets' safety

children.content: One woman has told the ABC she may have never left her abusive former partner if she had not been able to secure a safe home for her dog.

gallery.altText: Photos of a poodle cross dog.

thumbnail.altText: Photos of a poodle cross dog.

items.title: De Minaur and Hijikata bundled out of Queen's Club quarterfinals

children.content: Alex de Minaur and Rinky Hijikata both go down in the Queen's Club Championships quarterfinals as they prepare for Wimbledon.

image.altText: A man looks sad as he holds his tennis racquet on top of his head.

items.title: The women planting the seeds of a flower farm movement

children.content: Women looking for a new career or to invest in themselves are turning to micro flower farms, but pricing their blooms against industry giants can be challenging.
